items.title: Loading Data
items.description: CSV and parquet loads produce the DataFrames you group and merge; dtypes and parse dates set up valid join keys and time-safe merges.

items.title: Cleaning & Transformation
items.description: Normalized keys, deduplicated IDs, and consistent dtypes prevent silent merge failures and accidental many-to-many explosions during joins.

items.title: Data Leakage
items.description: Join leakage (global group stats, label tables, future timestamps) is a concrete instance of preprocessing leakage; this topic owns the pandas join patterns that cause it.

items.title: Feature Creation
items.description: Entity-level aggregations and dimension enrichment via merge are primary feature-engineering steps after relational reshaping in pandas.

items.title: Experiment tracking
items.description: Logging row counts before and after merges and documenting join keys helps reproduce pipeline steps when offline metrics shift after a join change.
